project_overview.project_name: Governance Modernization to Enable Efficient Service Delivery Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve government institutional capacity to mobilize and manage public financial and human resources efficiently
project_overview.beneficiaries: An estimated two million civil servants of Ethiopia will be direct beneficiaries. Taxpayers would benefit from better taxpayer service and risk management and citizens through more transparency in public finances and investment decisions. Indirectly, the project benefits all of Ethiopia.

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - Recent conflicts in Tigray, Amhara, Afar, and parts of Oromia have created significant constraints to effective service delivery in these areas

key_components.description: Focus on developing evidence-based and data-informed approach to HRM, competency-based HRM practices, institutionalizing training and capacity building, and reform innovation facility for line ministries
Support tax administration reforms through leveraging technology and data analytics for risk-based tax administration and improving quality and availability of taxpayer services
Support scaling up use of technology for improved PFM, address critical PFM deficiencies, and institutionalize professional training and capacity building
Support management, coordination, and M&E of project activities and funds including hiring key technical staff, M&E mechanisms, engagement surveys, and knowledge sharing

implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee comprising heads of MoF, MoR, and CSC; Project Management Coordination and Support Office (PMCSO) within MoF; Project Implementation Units (PIUs) in CSC, MoR, and MoF; Council for Good Governance in Prime Minister's Office providing oversight
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Procurement under the Project will be carried out in accordance with the World Bank's Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers. PIUs will be established and staffed under each implementing agency to manage project procurement activities
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Results Framework with project-specific indicators. CSC, MoR, and MoF with support of M&E experts in PMCSO will develop M&E system drawing on administrative and survey data streams

identified_risks.risk_description: Recent conflicts in Tigray, Amhara, Afar, and parts of Oromia have created significant constraints to effective service delivery
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project can contribute to addressing intractable drivers of conflict through investments in building civil service capabilities and improved efficiency and transparency of PFM and procurement systems

identified_risks.risk_description: High external and domestic debt, severe foreign exchange shortages, rising inflation, declining tax-to-GDP ratios
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project expected to expand revenue and fiscal space through creating more efficient civil service and more effective DRM and PFM systems

identified_risks.risk_description: Two of the implementing agencies (MoR and CSC) have no previous experience in implementing World Bank or donor financed projects
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project will finance support to strengthen governance, accountability, and coordination structures. Close coordination required amongst implementation agencies with clear definition of roles and responsibilities

identified_risks.risk_description: Risks related to delays in AWP&B, budget utilization, weak internal control, weak internal audit capacity, low staffing capacity, delays in IFRs
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Ensuring adequate staffing, sufficient capacity building, closer implementation support, establishing stringent internal control measures, allocating resources for internal audit and training

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited E&S risk management capacities within implementing entities, potential risks from digital solutions and ICT infrastructure generating e-wastes
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Hiring experienced environment and social specialists, ensuring procurement of ICT infrastructure follows green norms and environmental guidelines

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS 1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS 10: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
ESS 3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS 7: Indigenous Peoples/Sub-Saharan African Historically Underserved Traditional Local Communities
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Communities and individuals may submit complaints to existing project-level grievance mechanisms or the Bank's Grievance Redress Service (GRS)
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Project will collect and institutionalize gender disaggregated data on civil servants, develop National Human Resources Development Strategy with evidence-based actions for hiring, retention, and promotion of women, pilot implementation in two ministries with mentorship circles and leadership training for women

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project recognizes recent conflicts in Tigray, Amhara, Afar, and parts of Oromia have created constraints to service delivery. Project aims to address some intractable drivers of conflict through improved civil service capabilities and transparency
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia faces significant macroeconomic pressures, debt vulnerabilities, fiscal constraints, depleted liquidity buffers, foreign-exchange shortages, further exacerbated by internal conflict, COVID-19 pandemic, and drought. Growing fiscal pressures coupled with fragmented approach to public institutional reforms are root causes requiring urgent attention
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Main climate risks include floods and intense rainfall which could affect servers and civil servant activities. Climate risk assessment will be done to select appropriate locations of IT equipment
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Internal displacement of populations due to conflict has led to significant diversion of resources towards security and humanitarian efforts which has limited space for institutional reforms
